items.title: Jacobian as sensitivity table
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  inputs["inputs x1 ... xn"] --> J["Jacobian J: m x n"]
  J --> row1["row i: gradient of output fi"]
  J --> approx["delta f approx J delta x"]
items.caption: Each row of J lists how one output component responds to all input coordinates.

items.title: Chain rule through composed maps
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  x["x"] --> f["f(x)"]
  f --> h["h(f(x))"]
  Jf["J_f(x)"] --> Jg["J_{h o f} = J_h J_f"]
  Jh["J_h(f(x))"] --> Jg
items.caption: Composing functions multiplies Jacobians—this is backpropagation in matrix form.

items.title: Hessian eigenvalues and loss surface shape
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  crit["critical point: grad f = 0"] --> H["Hessian H"]
  H --> pos["all eigenvalues positive"]
  H --> neg["all eigenvalues negative"]
  H --> mix["mixed signs"]
  pos --> min["local minimum"]
  neg --> max["local maximum"]
  mix --> saddle["saddle point"]
items.caption: At a critical point, Hessian eigenvalues classify local curvature on the loss surface.
